sentence_reviews.text: Verified agentic systems place formal checks, typed actions, runtime monitors, permissions, and release gates around agents that can modify software or external state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Formal-methods, NIST AI RMF, structured-output, and systems references support checks, monitors, permissions, and gates around consequential agents; software-modification specificity is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: They define a deployable pattern for giving agents consequential authority without treating generated actions as trusted by default.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: NIST and formal-methods references support risk-governed deployment and not trusting AI outputs by default; the deployable-pattern wording is synthesis.
